William Friedman
wfried@umich.edu | (561) 866-0780 | www.linkedin.com/in/william--friedman

Analytical economics student who brings experience in strategy, financial modeling, and data driven problem solving across private investments and consulting exposure programs. Proven ability to break down ambiguous questions, conduct rigorous quantitative analysis, and communicate insights to senior stakeholders.

Applied Linear Regression in R to analyze key variables impacting car prices (horsepower, RPM, weight, body type)
Conducted quantitative analysis to determine the marginal effect of technical specifications on final market price, culminating in a detailed written report and data visualization.

Led and executed the hiring process for a new Assistant Coach, including recruitment, interviewing, and selection.
Managed a 7-member Executive Board and served as primary liaison for prospective players, overseeing outreach and onboarding.

Vice President of Operations and Finance

Managed and built an annual operating budget of ,000 using Google Sheets, allocating funds for travel, uniforms, ice time, coaching, and equipment.

jobText:
We are the leading provider of professional services to the middle market globally. As a Strategy and Management Consulting intern at RSM, you will work with mid-sized businesses in diverse industries and receive hands-on client experience with direct access to executive management.

Responsibilities:
Meet with clients and conducting management consulting projects based on client needs
Facilitate user workshops to gather and document business needs, requirements, weaknesses and challenges
Assist business users, project managers and IT leadership in optimizing the scope, benefits and risks of proposed projects
Conduct benchmarking research and analysis for IT spending, trends and industry practices
Provide leadership in working with solution vendors
Prepare reports on consulting services performed for clients, delivering reports where appropriate and discussing results and recommendations with clients
Work with clients to provide analysis, development and documentation of improvement opportunities
Complete client projects effectively and efficiently and maintaining a high level of proficiency
Travel to client locations as required

Basic Qualifications:
Working towards a BA or BS degree in Economics or related field
Travel to assigned client locations locally and nationally is required
Ability to travel up to 50%
A minimum 3.5 GPA is preferred
